title: Cystopus flavescens
taxonomy: Cystopus flavescens, also known as the yellow marshwort, is a species of flowering plant. It is classified within the family Cystopteridaceae, which is a family of ferns and their allies. The family Cystopteridaceae is a small group of plants that are closely related to the Osmundaceae family. Cystopus flavescens is further classified as a member of the genus Cystopteris, which includes several species of ferns. The specific classification of Cystopus flavescens is Cystopteris flavescens, a species of fern native to North America.
uses: Cystopus flavescens has been traditionally used in some parts of the world for its potential medicinal properties. It is often used in herbal remedies to treat various health issues, including skin conditions and inflammation.
